subject: Product Management
title: Trigger Events
description: A quick, practical guide to Trigger Events.

hook.text: A team using spreadsheets for project management hits a wall when they grow to 20 people.

buildup.text: Trigger events are moments when the status quo becomes painful enough that users actively seek alternatives.

discovery.text: Common triggers: team growth, new manager, funding round, compliance requirement, competitor pressure, or a painful incident.

twist.text: Products can't create trigger events — but they can be ready when triggers happen. Being top of mind at the right moment wins.

climax.text: The best product marketing identifies trigger events and targets messages to users experiencing them: 'Outgrown spreadsheets?'

punchline.text: You can't force the moment. But you can be there when it arrives.

quiz.question: What are trigger events in product adoption?
options: Moments when the status quo becomes painful enough that users actively seek alternatives
Marketing campaigns that force users to switch products
